item.title: Wikiwalk Junior Staff Post
item.content:encoded:
edit: edit made

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team.

+ Show Proposed edits
- Hide

To link to another text SCP, I propose the following edit:

If infection is widespread, refer to Special Procedures B-14, in the addendum.

to

If infection is widespread, refer to Special Procedures B-14, in the addendum. Should an instance of SCP-2976 become infected by SCP-3317, emergency procedure 451-Mill is to be enacted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
No idea what virtue signalling is, but sill think yer missing the point.

The only reason I put that in there was as a descriptor of the book, and its contents. I think you're reading way too much into this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Disagreed as it is completely irrelevant to the article, and to the reffered-to story. Considering even story details are absent this line exists only for the purposes of the authors wish to point out thier thoughts on the content of these sorts of stories, as opposed to being in any way significant.

Thus it is explicitly an excerisize in virtue signaling as the only reson the line exists is as the authors in real life attempt to disavow such things.

Point is its irrelevent and detracts from the story. This should be a clinical assesment of an object not someones platform for opinion…it really does break the entire thing irrepairably.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It could be more clinically written, but it's a fairly established fact that stories of this type from the time period were full of racial/sexist stereotypes. One sentence does not "preaching/virtue signaling" make.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Downvoted hard.

You have an otherwise wonderful article that is ruined by the authors insistence of preaching/virtue signaling. Retain a clinical NON OPINIONATED tone and this has the potential to be great. As it stands the offending section reads more as the authors opinion of older literature and attitudes, as opposed to a description of it.

Offending section is the first paragraph under the description. You have to describe an anomaly not judge a value system.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Nicely done, sir. I did not expect the effects of this SCP. Keep up the good work :)

+1

item.title: I FEEX
item.content:encoded: I noticed you mispelled a number so I FIX.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
For me the breaks in tone were jarring enough to turn an upvote to a downvote.

+ Tone problems explained
-The short version is avoid vernacular, contractions, idioms, ambiguity, and informal sentence structure...

…and focus on objective, precise, factual descriptions.

Not clinical tone and descriptions:

Merely being around other infectees is not enough to speed up infection; effort must be made to discuss SCP-2976.

The 'Hall' as described displays repeated abnormal structuring, with the dimensions, details and architecture being non-Euclidean in nature. Walls become floors, stairs lead to nowhere, and average humans who study the drawings can’t help but feel that something is fundamentally wrong with the pictures. There appears to be no underlying theme for the building, as modern, medieval, Oriental, Greek, and Arabic themes mix and mingle with no regard.

All of his books were widely panned by critics. In 1932, he wrote and paid to have SCP-2976 published. The publisher was a small, vanity press who seemed to have no idea what they were printing. SCP-2976 was seen as a major failure, and caused the publisher to go under. Mister Talont vanished after production and is presumed dead.

Clinical tone and descriptions:

Proximity to other infectees (hereafter designated SCP-2976-1) does not inherently accelerate infection; effort must be made between instances of SCP-2976-1 to discuss SCP-2976.

Collective reports and artistic renderings depict a structure consisting of architectural elements repeating without obvious pattern, often in illogical or non-functional arrangements. No consistent architectural style is displayed.1 Sanitized computer models constructed using consistently documented features as landmarks suggest a fixed structure occupying a Non-Euclidian region of space-time.

SCP-2976 was published in 1932 by a small vanity publisher at Mr. Talont's own expense. As with non-anomalous works by this author, SCP-2976 sold poorly and received little contemporary public attention beyond numerous negative critical reviews. Mr. Talont vanishes from public record following the failure of this final publication and is presumed dead.

See the difference?

Footnotes
1. One well documented region, for instance, features an amalgam of Modern, Medieval, Oriental, Greek, and Arabic elements.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Overall, I'm getting a vibe that sits somewhere between The King in Yellow and an Elder God cult. Talont would even have been a contemporary of Lovecraft (and possibly Chambers, depending on when he published his previous works), so that works in favor of said vibe. Not sure how intentional any parallels are, but there it is.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is great, I really enjoy the build-up and that it doesn't end up in some violent blood orgy of violence and blood.

Also changed all instances of amnesic to amnestic. I'd also consider just choosing a site for these books to be held in and unblackening them in the containment procedures.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The problem ends up being that everyone seems to have a different view on what words are actually clinic, aye? I struggled a lot on this one to try and use the best words for the situation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Some of the language isn't as clinical as I'd like, but overall I quite enjoyed this. It has a bit of the old-school Series I feel to it, which I like.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
One question. You say there have been two Stage 5 incidents in the description of Stage 5, but then say that there's only been one infection at the very end of the Description. Which is it?

+1
